title: A Baby Shower For Information As An Asset Class
gist: Paywall teaser: Didier Darcet flags that before year-end the CME and ICE are expected to launch one or two US futures contracts on computing power, backed by OpenAI, Anthropic, AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, data-center operators and BlackRock — which he frames as the 'baby shower' for compute/information as a new asset class. Light card — intro only.

themes.theme: Compute futures as new asset class

insights.claim: Before year-end 2026, one—possibly two—US futures contracts on computing power are expected to launch on the Chicago Mercantile Exchange and the Intercontinental Exchange.
insights.speaker: Didier Darcet
insights.so_what: A tradable, hedgeable price for compute would let AI buyers and data-center operators manage cost exposure — a structural new market to watch.
insights.quote: the birth of one—possibly two—US futures contracts on computing power, listed on the Chicago Mercantile and the Intercontinental exchanges

insights.claim: Participants in the compute-futures effort span AI labs (OpenAI, Anthropic), cloud giants (AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure), data-center operators and financial institutions including BlackRock.
insights.speaker: Didier Darcet
insights.so_what: Buy-side, supply-side and financial sponsors all participating signals genuine liquidity potential, not a niche product.
insights.quote: artificial intelligence laboratories such as OpenAI and Anthropic, cloud giants like Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud and Microsoft Azure, data-center operators and financial institutions including BlackRock

insights.claim: Darcet frames the compute-futures launch as the 'baby shower' for a new asset class — information/computing power — whose significance extends far beyond a single new derivative.
insights.speaker: Didier Darcet
insights.so_what: Treating compute as an investable asset class reframes AI infrastructure as a commodity market — a lens for future allocation and hedging.
insights.quote: Consider it the baby shower, not yet the birth, but certainly the celebration, of a new asset class.
